# WinUI Product And RE Campaign Progress

Status: active
Last updated: 2026-05-22

## Current Phase

Goodies observer state repair on 2026-05-22 corrected stale top-level audit wording. The copied-profile focused input observer proof at `release/readiness/goodies_input_observer_runtime_proof_2026-05-08.md` is GREEN for the ordinary Goodies wall path: it reached `CFEPGoodies__ButtonPressed`, parsed a private CDB log with `buttonEventCount=213`, `navigationEventCount=16`, `inputPathObserved=true`, `debuggerSkippedCommandWarningCount=0`, `hiddenReturnIds=[]`, and observed normal ids `66, 67, 68, 69, 70, 74`. The remaining Goodies gap is now hidden, cheat, developer, direct-selection, or non-wall reachability for `71`, `72`, and `73`, not the normal wall skip proof.

WinUI installer-preflight wave on 2026-05-22 refreshed the trusted-install blocker evidence. `npm run test:winui-msix-trusted-install-probe` now reaches `guarded-blocked`: disposable publish/package/sign/public-cert export/CurrentUser `TrustedPeople` trust pass, `Add-AppxPackage` remains blocked with `0x800B0109`, and cleanup verifies no local probe package, generated certificate, or WinUI process remains. `tools/winui_installer_preflight.py` now requires `release/readiness/winui_msix_trusted_install_guarded_blocker_2026-05-22.md`, and `npm run test:winui-installer-preflight` still reports `guarded-not-ready`. Release accounting is `R0=3489 R2=0 R3=2 R4=18188`, with curated/public allowlist `3118`.

Wave750: unwind-continuation hardening is the latest completed static RE wave. The pass saved comments/tags/signatures for twenty-five adjacent compiler-generated SEH unwind cleanup callbacks from `0x005d24e0` through `0x005d270f`, including `Unwind@005d24e0`, `Unwind@005d2580`, `Unwind@005d25e0`, `Unwind@005d2630`, `Unwind@005d26e0`, and `Unwind@005d270f`. It made no renames, function-boundary changes, or executable-byte changes, and completed serialized dry/apply/read-back/final-dry evidence. The current queue reports `6098` functions, `4612` commented functions, `1486` commentless functions, `963` exact-undefined signatures, and `27` `param_N` signatures. The broad comment-backed proxy is `4612/6098 = 75.63%`, while the stricter clean-signature proxy is `4554/6098 = 74.68%`; both remain telemetry only. Actual Ghidra project backup is verified at `[maintainer-local-ghidra-backup-root]\BEA_20260522-193422_post_wave750_unwind_continuation_verified` with `19` files, `167906183` bytes, and `DiffCount=0`. The raw commentless head is `0x0042f220 CSPtrSet__Clear`; the next high-signal static queue head is `0x005d2730 Unwind@005d2730`.

WinUI primary-lane validation was rerun on 2026-05-22 with `cmd.exe /c npm run test:winui-primary-lane`. It built `OnslaughtCareerEditor.WinUI.slnx`, passed `86/86` AppCore tests, and passed `50/52` UI tests with the two catalog-dependent Asset Library tests skipped as expected. This confirms the current state repair did not proceed from a stale product-lane baseline.

Campaign state repair: this file had lagged at Wave653 while `developer_agent_state.json`, `documentation_agent_state.json`, `re_orchestrator_state.json`, the canonical mutation logs, and `reverse-engineering/binary-analysis/static-reaudit-campaign.md` had advanced through Wave750. ## Wave 430 - Ghidra CMCBuggy Wheel-Motion Correction

### Findings

- Fresh metadata/decompile/xref/instruction/tag/string/vtable/source-inventory review selected fifteen saved `CMCBuggy`, shared wheel-motion math, mesh-part token, and destructable-segment motion-controller functions at `0x00493020` through `0x00494ce0`.
- `0x004934f0` now records the `RET 0x50` wheel-update shape with twenty stack arguments after `this`, plus profiling, lazy init, heightfield normal sampling, child-wheel recursion, and cached transform-update evidence.
- `0x00494350` and `0x004944b0` are now shared `Mat34` / `Vec3` helpers rather than CMCBuggy-only helpers; first read-back caught the Ghidra hidden-`this` signature nuance and the corrective apply fixed it.
- `0x00494b00` corrects the older mesh-part token wording: the helper rejects names containing `Body`, `Axle`, or `Wheel`, while `0x00494b50` checks `WheelMotion`.

## Wave 422 - Ghidra Landscape Patch Owner / Signature Correction

### Findings

- Fresh metadata/decompile/xref/instruction/vtable-adjacent/tag review selected four terrain patch / vertex-buffer targets after Wave421.
- `0x0048f180` is corrected to `CLandscapeTexture__InvalidateTileMaskOrRefreshAll`; body evidence marks texture update state, fills the optional update buffer with `0xff`, or refreshes the full `0..63` tile range.
- `0x0048f1e0` is corrected to `CDXPatch__CreateGridVertexBuffer`; `RET 0x4` supports one stack `grid_step` argument and the body computes `(grid_step+1)^2` before calling `CVBuffer__Create`.
- `0x0048f210` is corrected to `CDXPatch__RebuildHeightGridVertexBuffer`; body evidence calls `CVBuffer__Lock`, samples height data, writes `0x14`-byte vertex rows, and unlocks.
- `0x0048f320` is corrected to `CDXPatch__RestoreAndRebuildIfDirty`; vtable `0x005e5114` slot 1 dispatches here, calls `CVBuffer__Restore`, and rebuilds when `+0x40` is nonzero.
- The refreshed live queue reports `6043` functions, `1667` commented functions, `4376` commentless functions, `1861` undefined signatures, and `1813` `param_N` signatures.
- Current telemetry proxies are comment-backed `1667/6043 = 27.59%` and strict clean-signature `1604/6043 = 26.54%`; these are not milestones.

## Wave 411 - Ghidra HUD Overlay Helpers Correction

### Findings

- Fresh metadata/decompile/xref/tag review selected nine helper functions directly adjacent to the Wave410 `CHud__RenderOverlayForViewpoint` dispatcher.
- Seven corrected helpers are called directly by `CHud__RenderOverlayForViewpoint`: world-target sprites, controller-slot status, 3D target markers, objective progress/heading, objective status, objective slot fill, and tactical radar contacts.
- `CHud__RenderTacticalRadarContacts` calls `HudOverlay__DrawSpriteQuad` repeatedly and calls `CHud__SelectMarkerTextureIndexByUnitFlags` for marker texture selection.
- `0x004857e0` is better bounded as owner-neutral `HudOverlay__DrawSpriteQuad`, because it is a cdecl wrapper around `CVBufTexture__DrawSpriteEx` with fixed depth `0.011`, not a CHud method.
- `0x00485830` was corrected to `int __thiscall CHud__SelectMarkerTextureIndexByUnitFlags(void * this, void * unit)` after instruction read-back showed one stack argument and `RET 0x4`.

## Wave 410 - Ghidra HUD Overlay Correction

### Findings

- Fresh metadata/decompile/xref/source-callsite/tag review selected the HUD overlay root and target-indicator chain at `0x00487bc0`, `0x004879e0`, and `0x00482590`.
- `CDXEngine__PostRender` calls `CHud__RenderOverlay(&DAT_008aa4e8)` at the source-aligned `HUD.RenderOverlay();` point, then later calls `CHud__PromotePendingHudComponent(&DAT_008aa4e8)`.
- `CHud__RenderOverlay` loops active viewpoints and dispatches `CHud__RenderOverlayForViewpoint`.
- `CHud__RenderOverlayForViewpoint` validates target/camera context, selects and clips viewpoint overlay state, applies HUD sprite render state, and dispatches target-indicator, controller-slot, objective, slot-fill, radar, and related overlay helpers.
- `CHud__RenderTargetIndicatorOverlay` handles active/last target context, split-screen placement, CHud texture refs, Thunderhead miniature context, generic projection sphere context, and target health bars.
- Stuart's source snapshot does not include the matching HUD implementation body, so exact source body identity and neighboring overlay helper ownership remain open.

## Wave 394 - Ghidra Heightfield / Static-Shadow Correction Tranche

### Findings

- Fresh metadata/decompile/xref/instruction/tag review selected `0x0047e8e0`, `0x0047ea20`, `0x0047eb00`, `0x0047eb80`, and `0x0047ec60`.
- `0x0047e8e0` builds heightfield color-gradient/fog metadata around the `+0x1038/+0x103c`, `+0x107c/+0x108c`, and `+0x13c4/+0x13d0` field families.
- `0x0047ea20` and `0x0047eb00` sample packed signed 16-bit height data through the `+0x1028` buffer, with the latter carrying the bilinear 9x9 tile interpolation.
- `0x0047eb80` was an already-saved intermediate static-shadow label with a stale calling convention; read-back after correction consumes the explicit `world_pos` fastcall parameter instead of `in_EDX`.
- `0x0047ec60` samples terrain normals from heightfield data and still carries a bounded/provisional `CMonitor` owner.

## Wave 388 - Ghidra Queue-Head Helper Correction Tranche

### Findings

- Fresh metadata/decompile/xref/instruction/callsite/tag review selected `0x004098c0`, `0x00409e60`, `0x0040d320`, `0x00414010`, and `0x0041ad10`.
- `CLine__VFunc_01_004098c0` is a CLine vtable-slot wrapper that forwards the ECX receiver plus four stack arguments to `dispatch_target` vfunc `+0x10`.
- `CGeneralVolume__ToDoubleIdentity` is an x87 identity-style float-to-double helper used by JetPart turn and GeneralVolume axis-input wrappers.
- `Mat34__MultiplyBasisToOut` supersedes the older CMCBuggy-only owner label; ECX is the left-hand basis, stack args are `out_basis` and `rhs_basis`, and EAX returns `out_basis`.
- `CMonitor__ClearCurrentTrackedEntryFlag60` calls `CBattleEngineWalkerPart__GetCurrentWeapon` and clears field `+0x60` on the current weapon/tracked entry when present.
- `Vec3__AddInPlace` supersedes the older CMCTentacle-only owner label; ECX is destination vector and one stack argument is `add_vec3`.

## Wave 387 - Ghidra Geometry / Collision Correction Tranche

### Findings

- Fresh metadata/decompile/xref/instruction/tag review selected `0x00479020`, `0x00479200`, `0x00479630`, and `0x00479770`.
- `CMeshCollisionVolume__IsDirectionInsideTrianglePrism` is called from `CMeshCollisionVolume__TestSweptSphereAgainstTriangleCore` and tests a candidate direction through signed edge/plane dot-test context.
- `Geometry__SelectClosestPointOnTriangleEdges` computes clamped projections across all three triangle edges and selects the nearest candidate point to the query point.
- `Geometry__RaySphereEntryDistance` normalizes `rayEnd - rayStart`, solves origin-centered sphere entry distance, and returns the retail sentinel when no positive entry is observed.
- `Geometry__DistanceOutsideAabb` is called from `CMeshCollisionVolume__TestSweptSphereAgainstBounds`; its all-three-axis AABB branch is recorded as retail instruction-sequence evidence, not an idealized formula.

## Wave 385 - Ghidra CGame / CDXGame Owner Correction Tranche

### Findings

- Fresh metadata/decompile/instruction/RTTI/vtable/source-context review selected `0x0046c210`, `0x0046c2b0`, `0x0046c2d0`, `0x00541f00`, `0x00541f10`, `0x00541f30`, and `0x00541120`, with `0x00541140` retained as a deferred boundary guard.
- `0x0046c210` is the CGame constructor body: it begins from IController vtable setup, initializes CGame-shaped fields, then installs the CGame vtable.
- `0x0046c2b0` and `0x0046c2d0` are CGame scalar-deleting destructor wrapper and destructor body, not generic vfunc/constructor-like targets.
- `0x00541f00`, `0x00541f10`, and `0x00541f30` are CDXGame lifecycle targets, not CFrontEndVideo base lifecycle targets.
- `0x00541120` is `CBinkOpenThread__ctor`; RTTI resolves vtable `0x005e5078` to `CBinkOpenThread`, and the adjacent `0x00541140` vtable-slot body remains deferred.

## Wave 383 - Ghidra CGamut / Frustum-Culling Correction Tranche

### Findings

- Fresh metadata/decompile/xref/instruction/callsite/tag/source-availability review selected four targets at `0x004741b0`, `0x00474260`, `0x004742a0`, and `0x00476a20`.
- The Stuart source tree does not currently provide the `gcgamut.cpp` source body, so the pass used retail Ghidra read-back as authority and kept exact layout/source-body claims out of scope.
- `CGamut__Init` writes grid/cell fields, allocates height and visibility buffers, and registers `cg_gamutlocked`, `cg_showgamut`, and `cg_renderimposters`.
- `CGamut__Destroy` is the CEngine shutdown cleanup path for the height and visibility buffers.
- `CGamut__ComputePlanes` remains the large frustum-plane/rasterization body called from `CGamut__Calculate`.
- `CGamut__Calculate` is called from `CDXEngine__Render`, honors `cg_gamutlocked`, stores camera position, builds frustum corners, calls `CGamut__ComputePlanes`, and fills visibility bytes from height ranges. Its argument names are intentionally conservative.

- Added `tools\ghidra_gamut_wave383_probe.py` and focused tests.

## Wave 359 - Ghidra UnitAI Tail / Guide-Line Signature Tranche

### Findings

- Fresh metadata/xref/instruction/tag/decompile review selected ten adjacent UnitAI tail, guide constructor, and dropship ground-trace targets.
- `0x00448170` was corrected from the misleading `CLine__ctor_like_00448170` label to `CDropship__TraceGroundAndSpawnThrusterDust`; the function is a stdcall `RET 0x8` helper that builds a stack-local CLine, traces/samples static heightfield context, and spawns dropship thruster dust from an effect point and transform.
- `0x0047e290` was corrected to `CGuide__ctor_base`; the function takes one stack `guideOwner` argument, installs the base vtable, stores owner context, copies owner fields, clears `+0x1c`, and returns `this`.
- `0x00415d70` was hardened as `CBoatGuide__ctor`, calling `CGuide__ctor_base(this, guideOwner)` and writing vtable `0x005d8d5c`.
- `0x00447bb0` read-back caught an initially over-broad signature shape; the final saved signature is `void __thiscall CUnitAI__GetOrGenerateCachedAnchorPoint(void * this, void * outAnchorPoint)`, matching `RET 0x4`.

- The first saved apply exposed a Ghidra calling-convention nuance: `__thiscall` receiver parameters need to be named `this` or Ghidra inserts an extra hidden receiver and shifts the intended first semantic parameter onto the stack.
- `CCylinder__ResolveCollisionVFunc02` is now backed by `0x005d88cc` slot `2` plus `CSphere__VFunc_02_004e4d70` caller evidence with four stack arguments and ECX as cylinder context.
- `CDamage__LoadDamageTexture` keeps ECX/`this` as a 12-byte texture-info record, not the full `CDamage` object.
- `CDamage__InsertCellEntry` and `CDamage__RemoveCellEntryByCoords` are backed by `ret 0x10` and `ret 0x0c` stack cleanup evidence.
- The refreshed live queue reports `5974` functions, `1098` commented functions, `4876` commentless functions, `1958` undefined signatures, and `2110` `param_N` signatures.

- Added `tools\ApplyCylinderDamageSignatureTranche.java`.

## Wave 335 - Ghidra ComponentBasedOn Copy Helper Signature Pass

### Findings

- Fresh metadata/decompile/xref/instruction/tag review revisited the Wave 334 excluded `0x00433390` helper.
- The function was already named `CComponentBasedOn__CopyFrom`; no rename or new boundary was needed.

## Wave 329 - Ghidra Career Save Helper Signature Tranche

### Findings

- Fresh metadata/decompile/xref/instruction/tag/source/save-format review selected 21 Career/save/Goodies helpers from `0x0041b770` through `0x00421970`.
- No function names changed; the tranche hardened saved signatures/comments/tags.
- `CCareer__ReCalcLinks` no longer carries stale god-mode wording. Its saved comment now says the world-500 branch reads career `mSlots` at `+0x240c` bits `29/30`, matching source slots `61/62`.
- `CCareer__SaveWithFlag` now has an explicit `dest` buffer parameter and a proof-boundary comment for `mCareerInProgress`, version word, fixed `CCareer` dump, active options entries, and `OptionsTail_Write`.
- `CCareer__GetSaveSize` now has a bounded save-size comment covering fixed-career size, active `0x20` options entries, and the `0x56` tail, with current Steam saves observed as `0x2714`.
- `CCareer__GetKillCounterTopByte_23F4` now explicitly says it reads the top byte at in-memory career offset `0x23f4` minus `0x80`; it is not the lower 24-bit kill-count payload and should not be used as a true-view save patch offset.

## Wave 305 - Ghidra BattleEngine ResetConfiguration Signature Tranche

- `CBattleEngineJetPart__ResetConfiguration` and `CBattleEngineWalkerPart__ResetConfiguration` were already source/decompile/xref-backed names, but their saved signatures still used `void * param_1`.

- `0x0040c720` kept the useful `CGeneralVolume__ResetAndSetActiveReader` behavior label, but instruction read-back showed `ret 0x4` while the saved decompile still exposed stale multi-parameter debt.
- The callee `0x00402020` was also wrong from an earlier core-helper pass: it had been saved as no-stack-argument `__fastcall`, but instruction read-back shows `ret 0x4`.
- Saved Ghidra now has `0x00402020` as `void __thiscall CGeneralVolume__ResetCooldownTimestamp(void * this, void * activeReaderTarget)` and `0x0040c720` as `void __thiscall CGeneralVolume__ResetAndSetActiveReader(void * this, void * activeReaderTarget)`.
- A follow-on adjacent BattleEngine tranche corrected five stale cloak/HUD/interpolation labels: `CBattleEngine__HandleCloak`, `CLockInfo__GetLockPercentage`, `CBattleEngine__PlayHudSampleByName`, `CBattleEngine__GetInterpolatedEulerOrientation`, and `CBattleEngine__GetInterpolatedAutoAimPos`.
- The refreshed queue reports `5866` functions, `501` commented functions, `5365` commentless functions, `2076` undefined signatures, and `2450` `param_N` signatures.

## Wave 289 - Ghidra GeneralVolume / ChangeWeapon Signature Tranche

### Findings

## Wave 270 - Ghidra Opening Animation Callback Rename

- The sole remaining current name-confidence wrapper tail was `0x00418090`.
- The checked body supports a behavior label: it resolves the `opening` animation via `FindAnimationIndex`, handles state field `+0x254`, starts animation via vcall `+0xf0`, and schedules timer field `+0x25c`.
- Mixed DATA table slot `0x005d9080` remains unresolved, and checked CCockpit RTTI tables at `0x005d94ac` and `0x005d9524` do not prove the target owner.
- Headless rename dry/apply corrected `0x00418090` from `FindAnimationIndex__Wrapper_00418090` to `OpeningAnimationStateCallback__StartOpeningIfPending`.

- Metadata, decompile, xref, mixed-table, unresolved type-name, CCockpit-table, and focused probe read-back verified the saved state.
- The current helper/wrapper/uncertain-owner name-confidence tail is now zero, but signatures, parameter/local names, types, tags, structures, exact owner/source identities, and runtime behavior remain broad open work.

## Wave 251 - Ghidra Signature-Candidate Correction Pass

### Findings

- The three remaining signature candidates from the first name-confidence tranche were not ready for signature hardening, but their saved wrapper names were weaker than the available source/decompile/caller evidence.
- `0x0040e280` source-aligns to the base `CInitThing::Load(short, CMEMBUFFER&)` loader: callers invoke it before reading derived init fields, and the current retail body is a version-gated base-field reader.
- `0x0040f140` source-aligns to `UBattleEngineConfigurations::ShutDown`: it clears the configuration count and frees the configuration-name pointer array, and is called from world shutdown.
- `0x0040f520` source-aligns to `CBattleEngineData::CBattleEngineData()`: it initializes pointer sets and owned BattleEngineData storage before later initialise/load calls.
- Adjacent saved names around `0x0040f590`, `0x0040f890`, and `0x0040f980` now look like likely `CBattleEngineData::Initialise`, `Shutdown`, and `Load(CMEMBUFFER&)` candidates and need a separate correction pass before claiming that owner cluster is clean.
- The refreshed whole-database queue still has `5863` functions, while commented functions increased to `346`, commentless functions dropped to `5517`, and address-suffixed wrapper names dropped to `21`.

### Fixes Completed

- Added `tools/ghidra_signature_candidate_correction_probe.py` and fixture tests.
- Added `npm run test:ghidra-signature-candidate-correction`.
- Applied saved Ghidra renames:
  - `0x0040e280`: `DXMemBuffer_ReadBytes__Wrapper_0040e280` -> `CInitThing__LoadFromMemBuffer`
  - `0x0040f140`: `OID_FreeObject__Wrapper_0040f140` -> `BattleEngineConfigurations__ShutDown`
  - `0x0040f520`: `CSPtrSet_Init__Wrapper_0040f520` -> `CBattleEngineData__ctor`
- Applied saved proof-boundary comments to all three corrected functions.
- Added public-safe readiness at `release/readiness/ghidra_signature_candidate_correction_2026-05-09.md`.
- Updated release checklist, static re-audit queue/tranche docs, function indexes, mutation ledgers, and campaign state.

## Wave 231 - Cheat-Code Doc ASCII Normalization

### Findings

- The active Steam cheat-code docs used status glyphs, a curly apostrophe, an arrow glyph, and accented `latete` renderings that display poorly in this Windows shell.

## Wave 229 - Goodies Focused Input-Observer Runtime Proof

### Findings

- The focused observer copied-profile run completed against the ignored copied/windowed profile.

- After parser correction, the same private CDB log passed `--check-normal-skip` with `NORMAL_SEQUENCE_CONFIRMED`.
- Public-safe summary: `coordinateSampleCount=0`, `buttonEventCount=213`, `navigationEventCount=16`, `inputPathObserved=true`, `debuggerSkippedCommandWarningCount=0`, `hiddenReturnIds=[]`, and observed normal ids `66, 67, 68, 69, 70, 74`.

## Wave 227 - Goodies Selection-Observer Local Attach Runtime Attempt

### Findings

- CDB direct local `-p <pid>` attach can log and detach cleanly against the copied profile.
- CDB `-server ... -p <pid>` exits before opening the log in the current WinDbg package, so remote server attach remains unproven for this workflow.
- Hyphenated TCP server passwords make CDB exit before logging; helpers should reject punctuation.
- After changing the helper to default to local attached logging, the copied-profile Goodies observer produced a CDB log and parser summary.
- The parsed runtime result is still YELLOW: `INCOMPLETE_SEQUENCE`, `coordinateSampleCount=1017`, `navigationEventCount=0`, `hiddenReturnIds=[]`, and `expectedNormalSequenceObserved=false`.
- The parser observed rendered coordinate returns including `66`, `67`, and `68`, but not `69`, `70`, or `74`; the desired normal-skip sequence remains unproven.

### Fixes Completed

- Updated `tools/start_cdb_server.ps1` to default to local attached logging, keep remote server mode behind `-EnableRemoteServer`, validate CDB TCP passwords, and emit a JSON status line with the target PID, CDB PID, log path, and remote-server state.
- Added the same password validation to `tools/connect_cdb_client.ps1`.
- Updated the WinDbg/CDB runbook mirrors, Goodies observer plan, YELLOW runtime attempt note, release checklist, durable lessons mirrors, and state files.

### Validation Commands

- Direct copied-profile CDB attach/detach diagnostic - PASS; `-logo ... -p <pid> -c qd` exited `0` and created a log.
- Remote-server attach diagnostic - FAIL/expected; `-server ... -p <pid>` exited `-2147024809` and created no log.
- `tools/start_cdb_server.ps1` invalid-password guard - PASS/expected failure before CDB launch.
- `tools/start_cdb_server.ps1 -ProcessId 1234 -Password goodiesobserver ... -PrintOnly` - PASS; preview uses local `-logo ... -p 1234 -noio -cf ...`.
- `tools/start_cdb_server.ps1 -ProcessId 1234 -Password goodiesobserver -EnableRemoteServer ... -PrintOnly` - PASS; preview keeps remote server explicit.
- `tools/connect_cdb_client.ps1 -Password goodiesobserver -PrintOnly` - PASS.
- Copied-profile Goodies observer local attach - YELLOW; CDB log/parser summary produced, but `--check-normal-skip` failed because the expected normal sequence was not observed.

## Wave 226 - Goodies Selection-Observer Runtime Helper Hardening

### Findings

- A copied-profile Goodies observer runtime attempt launched the copied profile, found the managed BEA window, sent the bounded Goodies navigation input sequence, and captured a post-navigation frame.
- The attempt did not create the expected CDB observer log, so the prepared parser had no runtime-selected Goodie ids to check.
- The failed log is setup/tooling evidence, not Goodies behavior evidence. The helper needed exact PID attach and log-readiness gating before proof input.

### Fixes Completed

- Added exact `-ProcessId` attach support to `tools/start_cdb_server.ps1`.
- The CDB helper now rejects ambiguous process-name attach when multiple BEA processes are running, verifies the requested PID matches the expected process name, waits for the debugger log, and aborts/cleans up if no log appears before the timeout.

- Updated the WinDbg/CDB runbook mirrors, Goodies observer plan, release checklist, release manifest, durable lessons mirrors, release profile outputs, public allowlist, and state files.

## Wave 206 - BattleEngine Source-To-Binary Gap Partial-Candidate Accounting

### Findings

- The source-to-binary gap probe still counted the augmented-weapon anchor as purely source-only after Waves 203-205 established partial retail candidate evidence.
- That flattened useful progress into the wrong bucket. The correct status is partial retail candidate pending exact identity: activation/depletion behavior has retail candidate evidence, but source method boundaries and runtime behavior remain unproven.

### Fixes Completed

- Updated `tools/battleengine_source_binary_gap_probe.py` to report partial retail candidates separately from anchors with no retail candidate.
- Updated `release/readiness/battleengine_source_binary_gap_2026-05-06.md` current status: source-only count is now `16`, partial retail candidate count is `1`.
- Added a durable lesson that gap probes should distinguish no retail candidate, partial retail candidate, exact identity, and runtime proof.

### Validation Commands

- `py -3 -m py_compile tools\battleengine_source_binary_gap_probe.py` - PASS.
- `cmd.exe /c npm run test:battleengine-source-binary-gap` - PASS; binary families `3/3`, source anchors `17/17`, source-only anchors `16`, partial retail candidates `1`.
- `cmd.exe /c npm run test:battleengine-logic-coverage` - PASS; source anchors `17/17`, doc anchors `3/3`.
- `cmd.exe /c npm run test:md-links` - PASS.
- `cmd.exe /c npm run test:doc-commands` - PASS; documented npm commands checked `848` after recording the validation rows.

### Lessons Recorded

- Gap probes should distinguish no retail candidate, partial retail candidate, exact identity, and runtime proof. A source anchor with partial Ghidra evidence should not stay lumped into "source-only" if that hides progress, but it also should not graduate to exact identity.

### Remaining Risks

- This wave is evidence accounting only. It does not add new Ghidra exports, rename functions, launch BEA, mutate Ghidra, or prove runtime behavior.

## Wave 205 - BattleEngine Augmented Weapon Caller-Context Bridge

## Wave 205 - BattleEngine Augmented Weapon Caller-Context Bridge

### Findings

- Fresh read-only caller decompile for `0x004081c0` / `CMonitor__Process` shows the candidate call sits inside a source-aligned activation/depletion cluster.
- The caller context checks the active flag, calls `0x0040de40` through the current `CMonitor__HandleTargetStateChangeAndHudPrompt` name when the meter reaches threshold, decrements the meter while active, and clears meter plus active flag at depletion.
- This strengthens the augmented-weapon behavior bridge, but it still does not prove retail source-method boundaries. The retail compiler may have inlined, split, or reorganized source `AugmentWeapon()`, `UnaugmentWeapon()`, and per-frame update-loop behavior.

### Fixes Completed

- Updated `tools/battleengine_augmented_weapon_candidate_readback_probe.py` to require caller-context tokens from the ignored caller decompile.
- Updated `release/readiness/battleengine_augmented_weapon_candidate_readback_2026-05-07.md` with the activation/depletion cluster caveat.
- Updated the BattleEngine function index and lore-book mirror with the caller-context bridge.
- Added a durable lesson that sibling source-behavior tokens should refine the remaining gap instead of triggering premature rename.

### Validation Commands

- Red note: `Select-String` for `decrement`, `depletion`, `zeroing`, or `caller context` in the readiness note returned no rows before this patch.
- Read-only Ghidra decompile export for `0x004081c0` - PASS; targets `1`, dumped `1`, missing `0`, failed `0`.
- `py -3 -m py_compile tools\battleengine_augmented_weapon_candidate_readback_probe.py` - PASS.
- `cmd.exe /c npm run test:battleengine-augmented-weapon-candidate-readback` - PASS; checks `6/6`.
- `cmd.exe /c npm run test:md-links` - PASS.
- `cmd.exe /c npm run test:doc-commands` - PASS; documented npm commands checked `836` after recording the validation rows.
- `py -3 tools\docsync_check.py` - PASS.
- `cmd.exe /c npm run test:repo-hygiene` - PASS; hygiene unit tests `29/29`, live repo hygiene PASS.
- `py -3 tools\release_curated_manifest.py --check` - PASS; selected files `1354`.
- `py -3 tools\release_profile_snapshot.py --check` - PASS; counts `R0=1385 R2=0 R3=2 R4=18187`.
- `cmd.exe /c npm run test:public-allowlist` - PASS; rows checked `1354`.

### Lessons Recorded

- When a caller decompile contains sibling source-behavior tokens, use that to refine the remaining gap instead of jumping straight to a rename. The right conclusion may be "activation/depletion behavior cluster found; source method boundaries still unresolved."

### Remaining Risks

- This wave does not rename `0x0040de40` or `0x004081c0`.
- This wave does not prove final owner/name/signature correctness, exact source method boundaries, runtime behavior, shield-damage meter gain, or weapon-fired stealth reset.
- This wave does not launch BEA, mutate Ghidra, or mutate the installed game.

### Next Inspection Target

Continue with a bounded static identity target such as shield-damage meter gain or weapon-fired stealth reset, or plan a separate rename/read-back wave only after object/signature evidence is strong enough.

## Wave 204 - BattleEngine Augmented Weapon Caller Caveat

## Wave 204 - BattleEngine Augmented Weapon Caller Caveat

### Findings

- A follow-up read-only xref export for candidate function `0x0040de40` found one current known caller: `0x00408582` inside `0x004081c0`, currently named `CMonitor__Process`.
- That caller path is valuable evidence, but it reinforces that `0x0040de40` should stay a candidate note rather than being renamed to `CBattleEngine::AugmentWeapon()` from the string/decompile evidence alone.

### Fixes Completed

- Updated `tools/battleengine_augmented_weapon_candidate_readback_probe.py` to require the caller-xref evidence.
- Updated `release/readiness/battleengine_augmented_weapon_candidate_readback_2026-05-07.md` with the caller caveat.
- Updated the BattleEngine function index and lore-book mirror to state that `CMonitor__Process` is the current known caller and owner/name/signature remain unresolved.
- Added a durable lesson that surprising caller paths must be documented before any rename.

### Validation Commands

- Red note: `Select-String` for `CMonitor__Process` in the readiness note returned no rows before this patch.
- Read-only Ghidra xref export for `0x0040de40` - PASS; one xref row from `0x00408582` in `0x004081c0` / `CMonitor__Process`.
- `py -3 -m py_compile tools\battleengine_augmented_weapon_candidate_readback_probe.py` - PASS.
- `cmd.exe /c npm run test:battleengine-augmented-weapon-candidate-readback` - PASS; checks `5/5`.
- `cmd.exe /c npm run test:md-links` - PASS.
- `cmd.exe /c npm run test:doc-commands` - PASS; documented npm commands checked `826` after recording the validation rows.
- `py -3 tools\docsync_check.py` - PASS.
- `cmd.exe /c npm run test:repo-hygiene` - PASS; hygiene unit tests `29/29`, live repo hygiene PASS.
- `py -3 tools\release_curated_manifest.py --check` - PASS; selected files `1354`.
- `py -3 tools\release_profile_snapshot.py --check` - initially caught stale profile artifacts after tracked docs/state edits.
- `py -3 tools\release_profile_snapshot.py` - PASS; counts `R0=1385 R2=0 R3=2 R4=18187`.
- `py -3 tools\release_profile_snapshot.py --check` - PASS; counts `R0=1385 R2=0 R3=2 R4=18187`.
- `cmd.exe /c npm run test:public-allowlist` - PASS; rows checked `1354`.

### Lessons Recorded

- Caller xrefs can strengthen or weaken a candidate. If a source-looking candidate is reached through a surprising current owner path, document that path explicitly and keep the rename blocked until the object/signature story is resolved.

### Remaining Risks

- This wave does not rename `0x0040de40`.
- This wave does not prove the object layout, source owner, exact signature, runtime behavior, or final method identity.
- This wave does not launch BEA, mutate Ghidra, or mutate the installed game.

### Next Inspection Target

Continue source-to-retail identity work by inspecting the surrounding caller context or by finding a separate candidate for `UnaugmentWeapon()`, meter decay, shield-damage meter gain, or weapon-fired stealth reset.

## Wave 203 - BattleEngine Augmented Weapon Candidate Read-Back

## Wave 203 - BattleEngine Augmented Weapon Candidate Read-Back

### Findings

- The source `CBattleEngine::AugmentWeapon()` anchor has distinctive source tokens: max meter, active flag, event-time writes, and `hud_weapon_augmented` playback.
- A read-only byte/string search found `hud_weapon_augmented` in the retail executable at VA `0x00623540`.
- A read-only Ghidra xref export found one xref to that string from `0x0040def7` inside current Ghidra function `0x0040de40` named `CMonitor__HandleTargetStateChangeAndHudPrompt`.
- A fresh read-only decompile of `0x0040de40` contains max-meter, active-flag, event-time, and HUD sample lookup/playback tokens. This makes it the strongest current augmented-weapon activation candidate, but not a final rename or runtime proof.

### Fixes Completed

- Added `tools/battleengine_augmented_weapon_candidate_readback_probe.py`.
- Added `npm run test:battleengine-augmented-weapon-candidate-readback`.
- Added `release/readiness/battleengine_augmented_weapon_candidate_readback_2026-05-07.md`.
- Updated the BattleEngine function index and lore-book mirror with a candidate read-back section for `0x0040de40`.
- Added a durable lesson that string/constant xref candidates should be documented before any rename and kept separate from final owner/name/signature proof.

### Validation Commands

- Missing npm script red step - PASS/expected failure before implementation.
- Read-only Ghidra xref export for `0x00623540` - PASS; one xref row from `0x0040def7` in `0x0040de40`.
- Read-only Ghidra decompile export for `0x0040de40` - PASS; targets `1`, dumped `1`, missing `0`, failed `0`.
- `py -3 -m py_compile tools\battleengine_augmented_weapon_candidate_readback_probe.py` - PASS.
- `cmd.exe /c npm run test:battleengine-augmented-weapon-candidate-readback` - PASS; checks `4/4`.
- `cmd.exe /c npm run test:battleengine-logic-coverage` - PASS; source anchors `17/17`, doc anchors `3/3`.
- `cmd.exe /c npm run test:battleengine-source-binary-gap` - PASS; binary families `3/3`, source anchors `17/17`, source-only anchors pending binary identity `17`.
- `cmd.exe /c npm run test:md-links` - PASS.
- `cmd.exe /c npm run test:doc-commands` - PASS; documented npm commands checked `814` after the lessons-ledger reinforcement.
- `py -3 tools\docsync_check.py` - PASS.
- `cmd.exe /c npm run test:repo-hygiene` - PASS; hygiene unit tests `29/29`, live repo hygiene PASS.
- `py -3 tools\release_curated_manifest.py --check` - initially caught stale public allowlist after adding the new probe script.
- `py -3 tools\release_profile_snapshot.py --check` - initially caught stale profile artifacts after tracked docs/release edits.
- `py -3 tools\release_curated_manifest.py` - PASS; selected files `1354`.
- `py -3 tools\release_profile_snapshot.py` - PASS; counts `R0=1383 R2=0 R3=2 R4=18187`.
- `py -3 tools\release_curated_manifest.py --check` - PASS; selected files `1354`.
- `py -3 tools\release_profile_snapshot.py --check` - PASS; counts `R0=1383 R2=0 R3=2 R4=18187`.
- `cmd.exe /c npm run test:public-allowlist` - PASS; rows checked `1354`.
- State/manifest JSON parse - PASS.
- `git diff --check` - PASS with known line-ending warnings only.
- `tasklist.exe /FI "IMAGENAME eq BEA.exe"` - PASS; no matching task running.

### Lessons Recorded

- When a source anchor includes a distinctive retail string or constant, use string xrefs plus fresh decompile tokens to build a candidate identity note before renaming. Strong candidate evidence is still not final owner/name/signature proof.
- Current Ghidra names are labels, not truth. Candidate notes should separate the current name, inferred source method, and uncertainty.
- String/data VA derivations should record file offset and VA/mapping assumptions so future xref exports are repeatable.
- Candidate discovery, rename/read-back, and runtime proof should stay as separate waves with separate gates.
- Probes that depend on ignored raw Ghidra exports should also enforce public "not proven" wording so private evidence does not become an overbroad claim.

### Remaining Risks

- This wave does not rename `0x0040de40`.
- This wave does not prove final owner/name/signature correctness or exact full control-flow identity for all `AugmentWeapon()` statements.
- This wave does not prove `UnaugmentWeapon()` identity, meter decay, shield-damage meter gain, weapon-fired stealth reset, runtime HUD/audio behavior, or projectile behavior.
- This wave does not launch BEA, mutate Ghidra, apply a rename map, or mutate the installed game.

### Next Inspection Target

Either prepare a deliberate rename/read-back plan for `0x0040de40` if evidence is accepted, or continue static identity discovery for `UnaugmentWeapon()`, meter decay, shield-damage meter gain, or weapon-fired stealth reset.

## Wave 202 - BattleEngine Weapon Source/Read-Back Bridge

### Findings

- Source augmented-weapon and weapon-fired stealth anchors already had source-only evidence, and weapon-effect/projectile helpers already had read-only Ghidra read-back evidence.
- The gap was the bridge boundary: helper read-back is related retail weapon-system evidence, but it does not prove exact augmented-meter identity, exact weapon-fired stealth-reset identity, or runtime firing behavior.
- Public readiness and function notes needed to say that boundary explicitly so future agents do not overclaim the evidence.

### Fixes Completed

- Added `tools/battleengine_weapon_source_readback_bridge_probe.py`.
- Added `npm run test:battleengine-weapon-source-readback-bridge`.
- Added `release/readiness/battleengine_weapon_source_readback_bridge_2026-05-07.md`.
- Updated weapon helper readiness and function notes to state the source/read-back bridge boundary.
- Mirrored function-note updates under `lore-book/`.
- Added a durable lesson that source/read-back bridge checks must keep related-helper proof, exact source identity, runtime behavior, and rebuild parity separate.
- Regenerated the curated public allowlist after the new public probe script was added.

### Validation Commands

- `py -3 -m py_compile tools\battleengine_weapon_source_readback_bridge_probe.py` - PASS.
- `cmd.exe /c npm run test:battleengine-weapon-source-readback-bridge` - PASS; checks `7/7`.
- `cmd.exe /c npm run test:battleengine-weapon-helper-ghidra-readback` - PASS; functions `2/2`.
- `cmd.exe /c npm run test:battleengine-logic-coverage` - PASS; source anchors `17/17`, doc anchors `3/3`.
- `cmd.exe /c npm run test:battleengine-source-binary-gap` - PASS; binary families `3/3`, source anchors `17/17`, source-only anchors pending binary identity `17`.
- `cmd.exe /c npm run test:md-links` - PASS.
- `cmd.exe /c npm run test:doc-commands` - PASS; documented npm commands checked `796`.
- `py -3 tools\docsync_check.py` - initially caught missing lore-book function-note mirrors, then PASS after mirror sync.
- `cmd.exe /c npm run test:repo-hygiene` - PASS; hygiene unit tests `29/29`, live repo hygiene PASS.
- `py -3 tools\release_curated_manifest.py --check` - initially caught stale public allowlist after adding the probe script.
- `py -3 tools\release_curated_manifest.py` - PASS; selected files `1353`.
- `py -3 tools\release_curated_manifest.py --check` - PASS; selected files `1353`.
- `py -3 tools\release_profile_snapshot.py --check` - PASS; counts `R0=1381 R2=0 R3=2 R4=18187`.
- `cmd.exe /c npm run test:public-allowlist` - PASS; rows checked `1353`.
- State/manifest JSON parse - PASS.
- `git diff --check` - PASS.
- `tasklist.exe /FI "IMAGENAME eq BEA.exe"` - PASS; no matching task running.

### Lessons Recorded

- Source/read-back bridge checks should require public readiness and function notes to state proof boundaries explicitly. Related retail helper evidence can support a bridge, but it must not silently become proof of exact source identity, runtime behavior, or rebuild parity.

### Remaining Risks

- This wave does not prove exact Steam retail augmented-meter control-flow identity.
- This wave does not prove exact Steam retail weapon-fired stealth-reset control-flow identity.
- This wave does not prove runtime firing, projectile, stealth, or augmented-weapon behavior.
- This wave does not apply a rename map, mutate Ghidra, mutate `BEA.exe`, or launch BEA.

### Next Inspection Target

Continue one exact BattleEngine source-to-retail identity question at a time, or schedule a copied-profile runtime proof only after writing a bounded proof matrix and ensuring the original install remains read-only.

## Baseline

- Branch: `wip/sandbox`
- Starting HEAD: `2425c691ac7580fdd248915181530ba7c8e1baf4`
- Starting working tree: clean.
- Local retail install supplied by user: `C:\Program Files (x86)\Steam\steamapps\common\Battle Engine Aquila`
- Install posture: read-only source material only; copied profiles/app-owned artifact roots are required for mutation or proof work.

## Findings

- Root scripts and README are WinUI-first after the prior consolidation work.
- Patch Bench already uses an app-owned working copy for executable patching.
- The WinUI shell still exposed a global `Launch Game` action that started the configured retail `BEA.exe` directly. Launching the real install is not a file patch, but it is not consistent with the current read-only-install posture because the retail game can update local/global state.
- Save Lab visual QA showed that the app no longer needed to start there by default, but the Save Lab page itself still needed a calmer first-run task guide and deterministic analyzer capture.
- Settings visual QA showed the configured install as a full local path in the default view instead of a friendly read-only-source summary.
- Media visual QA previously captured a loading/empty state; the Media page needed enough deterministic visual coverage to show configured-directory audio and video catalog states without primary path exposure.
- WinUI visual smoke attempted to isolate `APPDATA`, but `AppConfig` reads the Windows known-folder path; without an explicit config-root override, the visual smoke could still inherit the real user config.
- A disposable unpackaged `dotnet publish` output initially omitted `OnslaughtCareerEditor.WinUI.pri`, causing the published executable to exit before a main window appeared.
- Published visual screenshots showed Patch Bench still exposed the full source executable path in the primary workflow column.
- Media visual screenshots proved the catalog ready state but not actual playback interaction; real audio/video interaction needed a focused desktop UI smoke.
- The WinUI Media page had named XAML fields, but it lacked stable UI Automation IDs for precise automated interaction.
- The asset extraction pipeline still described future Electron workbench integration, which is stale after the WinUI-first lane reset.
- Before building a WinUI texture/model surface, the bounded backend extraction path needed fresh proof against the read-only local install.
- The primary WinUI project has a clear dependency surface with Windows App SDK, WebView2, NAudio, LibVLCSharp, and VideoLAN.LibVLC.Windows.
- NuGet advisory checks report no vulnerable packages for the WinUI project or retained support/parity solution from the configured sources.
- NuGet reports newer WinUI direct package versions, including a major Windows App SDK update. These should be handled in a separate compatibility slice rather than folded into a release-review documentation wave.
- LibVLCSharp and VideoLAN.LibVLC.Windows carry LGPL-2.1-or-later license signals and need explicit notice/compliance review before any public binary redistribution.
- `OnslaughtCareerEditor.Release.slnx` remains a support/parity solution and intentionally does not include WinUI; release docs already require explicit WinUI project build/publish commands.
- The initial full-corpus run's mesh failures were not stable file-level failures: isolated sample/range runs succeeded, and full loose/embedded mesh lanes succeeded when run separately.
- A parallel loose/embedded mesh experiment produced a file-lock failure against the legacy extractor template file, confirming the asset lanes should run serially.
- The updated split-lane orchestrator completed a full run with 847/847 textures, 213/213 loose meshes, 139/139 embedded meshes, full language/video inventory, and 3,817 catalog entries.
- The proven asset catalog had no native WinUI product surface before this wave.
- The first Asset Library visual smoke showed the page was too path-heavy/cramped, so the loaded state needed to hide the full catalog path and make loaded rows visible.
- Release docs had dependency/license inventory evidence, but they still treated third-party notices and LGPL redistribution review as manual open work with no deterministic notice draft/check command.
- Restored project assets and local NuGet metadata can produce a public-safe notice draft without committing local package payloads, private paths, or private game evidence.
- WinUI has no existing native 3D model renderer, and generated model exports are binary FBX by default, so claiming in-app 3D parsing/rendering would be risky and dishonest in a small slice.
- Asset Library could still give users an immediate model-inspection path by opening generated `.fbx` exports in a local viewer while keeping local paths collapsed and action scope restricted.
- NuGet reported non-major updates for LibVLCSharp, WebView2, NAudio, and VideoLAN.LibVLC.Windows plus a major Windows App SDK 2.x update.
- The non-major runtime/media updates could be validated safely in one slice, while Windows App SDK 2.x should remain a separate platform migration.
- Windows App SDK 2.x then built cleanly in the WinUI project and passed debug-build, published-output launch/visual, and published-output Media interaction smoke.
- The notice draft existed and passed drift checks, but publish output still needed an explicit inclusion target so binary folders carry a copy.

## Fixes Completed

- Added this campaign goal and progress/evidence ledger.
- Replaced the global WinUI shell `Launch Game` action with `Review Setup`.
- The shell action now navigates to Settings and explains the read-only install/app-owned working-copy posture instead of starting `BEA.exe`.
- Added a static UiTest guard so the shell action cannot regress back to direct `Process.Start`/retail-executable launch behavior unnoticed.
- Added an explicit FlaUI visual smoke that launches the WinUI app, captures primary screens under ignored `subagents/winui-visual-qa/2026-05-05/`, and leaves no app process running.
- Added a public-safe WinUI visual QA note at `release/readiness/winui_visual_qa_2026-05-05.md`.
- Added a dedicated WinUI Home page with task routing for Save Lab, Media, Lore, Patch Bench, Settings, and About.
- Updated the shell to include Home as the first navigation item and use `LastTab = -1` as the Home/start sentinel without shifting existing persisted tab numbers.
- Regenerated visual QA screenshots after adding Home and updated the public-safe visual review table.
- Added a Save Lab task guide with direct actions for analyzing a file, editing a copied save, and reviewing options.
- Added a test-only Save Lab initial-subtab override for visual QA so screenshots prove the first-run analyzer state instead of inheriting a prior persisted subtab.
- Updated detected-file dropdown placeholder copy so available files say "Choose..." instead of "No detected..." when no item is selected.
- Regenerated the Save Lab visual screenshot and updated the public-safe visual QA table.
- Reworked Settings so the configured game install shows as a folder summary with read-only source-material copy, while the full install path and config path are collapsed under details.
- Regenerated the Settings visual screenshot and updated the public-safe visual QA table.
- Reworked Media source cards so audio/video show configured-install summaries and path details are collapsed by default.
- Changed selected audio/video summaries to show source group, filename, duration/size instead of full paths.
- Added deterministic visual-smoke routing for Media audio and Media video tabs, and regenerated separate private screenshots for both.
- Added `ONSLAUGHT_APP_CONFIG_ROOT` support in `AppConfig` and covered it with an AppCore test.
- Updated native WinUI visual smoke to use an ignored config root under `subagents/` and write any detected game path only into ignored test config.
- Added a WinUI publish target that copies the app PRI resource into the publish output.
- Added `ONSLAUGHT_WINUI_TEST_EXE_PATH` support to WinUI launch/visual smoke tests so they can validate a published executable.
- Added launch-smoke config isolation under ignored `subagents/`.

- Recorded public-safe published-notice evidence in `release/readiness/winui_published_notice_inclusion_2026-05-06.md`.

## Validation

Targeted WinUI build, active UiTests, disposable publish, published launch smoke, published visual smoke, focused Media interaction smoke, Windows App SDK 2.x migration smoke, published-notice inclusion smoke, bounded asset backend smoke, full-corpus split-lane asset backend smoke, WinUI Asset Library visual smoke/export actions/model metadata preview/model wireframe preview/full-corpus model preview coverage, third-party notice drift check, Python syntax, state JSON parsing, repo hygiene, public allowlist, release profile, curated manifest, docsync, markdown links, documented commands, and whitespace validation are green. See `.codex/state/winui-product-re-campaign-evidence.md`.

## Remaining Risks

- First native WinUI screenshot QA exists, but deeper manual/product critique and follow-up fixes remain.
- The screenshot review is still YELLOW overall: texture/model coverage, packaging/signoff, broader media row coverage, and broader RE coverage need focused follow-up.
- Disposable unpackaged WinUI publish smoke is complete; signed installer/MSIX/install-uninstall/trust proof is not complete.
- Full texture, loose-mesh, embedded-mesh, language/video inventory, and full catalog generation are proven through the split-lane backend. WinUI generated-catalog browsing, model metadata, and a bounded in-app wireframe preview are proven; full native 3D model rendering remains unproven.
- Dependency/license review, source-controlled notice drafting, LGPL redistribution checklist, non-major dependency refresh, Windows App SDK 2.x migration, and published-output notice inclusion are complete at release-evidence level; legal/compliance approval and future package-upgrade compatibility testing remain unproven.
- Asset Library can open generated `.fbx` exports through the OS viewer and show lightweight binary FBX metadata facts plus a bounded in-app wireframe preview, but full native in-app 3D model rendering, camera controls, animation, and materials remain unproven.
- Direct WinUI packages are current against configured NuGet sources after Windows App SDK 2.x migration; signed installer/MSIX/trust proof remains unproven.

## Next Inspection Target

Continue with the next high-value WinUI/product gap: design native in-app 3D model rendering deliberately, plan installer/MSIX/signing proof, or continue RE logic reconstruction work.

## Wave 20 - WinUI Model Metadata Preview

### Findings

- Current generated model exports are binary FBX files, not a renderer-ready public fixture format.
- Private ignored catalog inspection found the current split-lane catalog shape still exposes texture, loose-mesh, and embedded-mesh rows with local export paths.
- A lightweight read-only FBX metadata reader is feasible because the exported files expose standard binary FBX node names such as `Geometry`, `Vertices`, and `PolygonVertexIndex`.
- A full native 3D renderer remains a separate product/packaging decision; this wave should not add an unreviewed rendering dependency.

### Fixes Completed

- Added `FbxModelSummaryReader` and `AssetModelSummary` to AppCore.
- Asset catalog mesh rows now include model summary facts for existing generated `.fbx` exports.
- WinUI Asset Library selected model rows now show a compact visible model-facts line and a fuller metadata panel, while still saying native in-app 3D rendering is not enabled yet.
- Fixed Asset Library tab style lookup to use app resources instead of page-local resources.
- Extended visual smoke so Asset Library captures separate texture and model screenshots with public-safe synthetic fixtures.
- Recorded public-safe evidence in `release/readiness/winui_model_metadata_preview_2026-05-06.md`.

### Remaining Risks

- Full native in-app 3D rendering, material fidelity, camera controls, animation/skeleton handling, and packaged-output model metadata behavior remain unproven.
- The current metadata reader is intentionally narrow: it reads binary FBX header/node counts and does not attempt full FBX scene reconstruction.

### Next Inspection Target

Continue with installer/MSIX/signing planning, native 3D renderer dependency review, or deeper RE logic reconstruction.

## Wave 21 - WinUI Installer/Signing Preflight

### Findings

- The WinUI project is intentionally configured as unpackaged/self-contained (`EnableMsixTooling=false`, `AppxPackage=false`, `WindowsPackageType=None`, `WindowsAppSDKSelfContained=true`).
- Prior evidence proves disposable unpackaged publish output, not signed installer/MSIX readiness.
- Release docs already named signed installer/MSIX/install-uninstall/trust proof as unproven, but there was no focused machine-checkable guard for this posture.

### Fixes Completed

- Added `tools/winui_installer_preflight.py`.
- Added `npm run test:winui-installer-preflight`.
- Added a WinUI product-lane static test that guards the current unpackaged posture and preflight script.
- Documented the new gate in release/signoff docs and status docs.
- Recorded public-safe evidence in `release/readiness/winui_installer_signing_preflight_2026-05-06.md`.

### Remaining Risks

- This wave does not create or sign an installer.
- MSIX, install/uninstall, certificate trust, SmartScreen posture, final binary notice inclusion, legal approval, and output-specific LGPL compliance remain unproven.

### Next Inspection Target

Continue with actual installer/MSIX candidate proof if signing/publishing inputs are available, or continue product work through native in-app model rendering or deeper RE logic reconstruction.

## Wave 22 - WinUI MSIX Property-Override Probe

### Findings

- The source tree has no source `Package.appxmanifest`, no `.wapproj`, and no WinUI package certificate properties.
- A disposable `dotnet publish` with `WindowsPackageType=MSIX` and signing disabled completed, but produced another unpackaged publish folder.
- No `.msix`, `.appx`, `.appinstaller`, `.msixbundle`, or `.appxbundle` files were produced.

### Fixes Completed

- Extended `tools/winui_installer_preflight.py` to report the missing source package manifest and packaging project as part of the current `guarded-not-ready` posture.
- Extended the WinUI product-lane static test to guard those preflight messages.
- Recorded public-safe MSIX probe evidence in `release/readiness/winui_msix_probe_2026-05-06.md`.
- Updated status/checklist docs to say the property override is not enough for real MSIX packaging.

### Remaining Risks

- Real MSIX/installer work still requires deliberate package identity, manifest/project setup, signing, install/launch/uninstall smoke, notice verification, and compliance review.

### Next Inspection Target

Continue with native in-app model rendering, deliberate installer package setup if signing strategy is available, or deeper RE logic reconstruction.

## Wave 23 - WinUI Model Wireframe Preview

### Findings

- The generated FBX fixture and current private exports expose binary FBX `Vertices` and `PolygonVertexIndex` arrays that can support a bounded geometry preview.
- A full renderer dependency is not needed for the next product step; a dependency-free wireframe preview gives users visible model feedback without overstating material/animation fidelity.
- The first wireframe UI placement was too low in the panel; visual smoke review forced moving it into the selected model area's first visible region.

### Fixes Completed

- Extended `FbxModelSummaryReader` to extract bounded uncompressed FBX vertex and polygon-index samples.
- Added `AssetModelGeometryPreview`, preview vertices, and preview edges to AppCore.
- Added WinUI Asset Library wireframe rendering in the selected model panel.
- Updated product-lane static checks and visual smoke screenshots to cover the model preview.
- Recorded public-safe evidence in `release/readiness/winui_model_wireframe_preview_2026-05-06.md`.

### Remaining Risks

- Full native 3D rendering, materials, textures, UVs, normals, animation, skeletons, camera controls, lighting, broader private model coverage, and packaged-output behavior remain unproven.

### Next Inspection Target

Continue with broader private model wireframe coverage, deliberate GLB/glTF/native rendering pipeline selection, or deeper RE logic reconstruction.

## Wave 24 - WinUI Model Preview Coverage

### Findings

- The first broad private coverage probe found 352/352 generated model FBX exports were present and metadata-readable, but only 24/352 produced wireframe edges.
- The non-wireframe rows still reported vertex and polygon index counts, which showed the reader was counting compressed FBX array lengths but not decompressing the coordinate/index payloads for preview.
- The current full-corpus split-lane catalog is sufficient to regression-check model preview coverage without committing private extracted FBX files.

### Fixes Completed

- Added bounded zlib-compressed FBX array support for `Vertices` and `PolygonVertexIndex`.
- Added `AssetModelPreviewCoverageService` to summarize catalog-level model preview coverage without exposing local paths.
- Added `inspect-asset-model-preview` to AppCore.Host as a read-only JSON diagnostic command.
- Added synthetic AppCore tests for compressed FBX arrays and catalog-level model preview coverage.
- Ran the read-only host command against the ignored private full-corpus split-lane catalog and recorded public-safe counts in `release/readiness/winui_model_preview_coverage_2026-05-06.md`.

### Remaining Risks

- Full native 3D rendering, materials, UV rendering, normals, animation, skeletons, camera controls, lighting, packaged-output model coverage, and public redistribution approval remain unproven.
- The current coverage command covers binary FBX generated by the existing extractor path; future exporter formats need their own validation.

### Next Inspection Target

Continue with deliberate model-renderer pipeline selection, installer/MSIX/signing planning, or deeper RE logic reconstruction.

## Wave 26 - WinUI Save Lab Empty-State Polish

### Findings

- The refreshed Save Lab screenshot still showed five blank analyzer metric cards under the first-run empty prompt.
- The first attempt hid only the small analyzer title grid because the metric cards lived in a separate unnamed grid.
- The visual-smoke screenshot was fresh enough to catch the bad state, so the issue was a real WinUI layout wiring bug rather than stale screenshot evidence.

### Fixes Completed

- Added an analyzer empty state that tells users to choose a save or options file before metrics appear.
- Hid both the analyzer header and the analyzer metric cards until a document is rendered.
- Restored both analyzer sections only when `RenderDocument` receives a real analysis result.
- Added product-lane static assertions for the empty-state copy and hidden analyzer grids.
- Refreshed the public-safe visual QA note so the Save Lab row no longer reports blank metric cards.

### Remaining Risks

- This wave improves the first-run Save Lab analyzer state only; it does not add deeper save editing features.
- Manual product critique is still useful for the full Save Lab editing workflow after a real save is selected.

### Next Inspection Target

Continue with deliberate model-renderer pipeline selection, installer/MSIX/signing planning, or deeper RE logic reconstruction.

## Wave 27 - WinUI Real Texture Preview Visual Smoke

### Findings

- The standard Asset Library screenshot used a synthetic texture, which proved rendering infrastructure but did not answer the user's concern that real textures could look black or unclear.
- The first real-catalog screenshot loaded a real generated catalog and selected a real row, but the loaded-catalog banner and secondary controls pushed the preview image below the first viewport.
- Real texture exports include dark and transparent images, so the preview canvas needs a user-selectable background instead of relying on the app's dark surface.

### Fixes Completed

- Compacted the loaded-catalog banner into a short summary row with `Change catalog` and collapsed path details on the right.
- Moved the texture preview above secondary actions/background controls so the selected texture is visible immediately.
- Added Neutral, Light, and Dark texture preview background controls.
- Added an explicit real-catalog visual smoke guarded by `ONSLAUGHT_WINUI_REAL_ASSET_CATALOG`.
- Ran that smoke against the ignored full generated catalog and captured a private screenshot of the real `f_trooperd` texture row.
- Recorded public-safe evidence in `release/readiness/winui_asset_texture_preview_visual_2026-05-06.md`.

### Remaining Risks

- This proves one representative real generated texture row and the standard synthetic visual path; it does not prove every texture row is visually rich.
- Public redistribution rights for extracted textures remain unproven and private assets stay ignored.
- Installer/MSIX packaged behavior remains unproven.

### Next Inspection Target

Continue with deliberate model-renderer pipeline selection, installer/MSIX/signing planning, or deeper RE logic reconstruction.

- Reduced the lower video-player layout from three primary cards to Now Playing plus Transport, giving the player surface more room.
- Reran focused Media playback interaction smoke and confirmed the selected video frame is larger/readable.
- Reran standard visual smoke and confirmed the Video tab still shows a clear player surface in the first viewport.
- Recorded public-safe evidence in `release/readiness/winui_media_video_player_layout_2026-05-06.md`.

### Remaining Risks

## Wave 30 - WinUI Save Editor Copied-Save Patch Smoke

### Findings

- The Save Editor had in-place blocking in AppCore and UI state, but no native desktop UI proof showed a copied real save could be patched through the WinUI page.
- The Save Editor result `InfoBar` was initialized collapsed and updated after patch attempts without being made visible.
- The first copied-save smoke attempt exposed automation flakiness from keyboard-style text entry: long paths could split across input/output fields when desktop focus changed.

### Fixes Completed

- Added stable automation IDs for Save Editor input/output paths, patch action, pending-changes text, safety hint, output log, detected files, and result banner.
- Made the Save Editor result banner visible for both blocked advanced-override states and patch results.
- Updated the real-save smoke helper to set TextBox values directly instead of sending path text as keystrokes.
- Added explicit `WinUiSaveAnalyzerInteractionSmokeTests.SaveEditor_PatchesCopiedRealSaveThroughUiWhenProvided`.
- Ran the copied-save patch smoke with a private `.bes` supplied through `ONSLAUGHT_WINUI_REAL_SAVE_PATH`; the smoke copied the input under ignored `subagents/`, patched only the copy to a distinct output, verified the copied input hash remained unchanged, and captured a private screenshot.
- Recorded public-safe evidence in `release/readiness/winui_save_editor_copied_save_smoke_2026-05-06.md`.

### Remaining Risks

- This proves one representative copied-save patch path; it does not prove every save variant or every advanced override combination.
- The output log still exists as a maintainer-friendly detail surface; broader user-facing wording polish can happen in a future Save Editor UX pass.
- Signed installer/MSIX behavior remains unproven.

### Next Inspection Target

Continue with deliberate model-renderer pipeline selection, installer/MSIX/signing planning, broader Save Editor workflow polish, or deeper RE logic reconstruction.

## Wave 31 - WinUI Patch Bench Copied-Executable Smoke

### Findings

- Patch Bench had first-viewport visual evidence and static copied-executable guards, but no native desktop smoke proved create/verify/apply/restore through the WinUI page.
- The first copied-executable smoke found a test dedupe bug: Windows case-insensitive file matching could count the same `BEA.exe` working copy twice.
- The next smoke found a restore wait-condition bug: the apply log included the word `Restore`, so the test could hash-check before the restore handler completed.
- The scrolled screenshot exposed a product UX issue: the primary Operation Log showed full private working-copy paths after apply.
- The user's current local source executable verifies as already patched for the selected stable patch set, so this wave proves copied-workflow safety and idempotent apply/restore rather than a first-time clean-to-patched transition.

### Fixes Completed

- Added stable automation IDs for Patch Bench source path, working-copy path, create-copy, verify, apply, restore, workflow hint, and operation log controls.
- Added explicit `WinUiPatchBenchInteractionSmokeTests.PatchBench_VerifiesAppliesAndRestoresCopiedExecutableWhenProvided`.
- Deduped working-copy discovery case-insensitively.
- Tightened restore waiting to require `Restore complete.` and matching working-copy hash.
- Summarized app-owned target/backup labels in the primary Operation Log while leaving full paths in collapsed details.
- Ran the smoke against the user's private local `BEA.exe` source supplied through `ONSLAUGHT_WINUI_REAL_BEA_EXE_PATH`; it created an ignored app-owned copy, verified selected patches, applied to the copy, restored the backup, and confirmed the source hash was unchanged.
- Captured a scrolled private screenshot of the Patch Bench action/output region.
- Recorded public-safe evidence in `release/readiness/winui_patch_bench_copied_executable_smoke_2026-05-06.md`.

### Remaining Risks

- This does not prove a first-time clean-retail-to-patched byte transition because the local source was already in known patched state for selected stable patches.
- It proves the selected stable Patch Bench path, not every optional/experimental patch or every executable specimen.
- Signed installer/MSIX behavior remains unproven.

### Next Inspection Target

Continue with deliberate model-renderer pipeline selection, installer/MSIX/signing planning, broader scrolled visual/accessibility review, or deeper RE logic reconstruction.

## Wave 32 - WinUI Scrolled Visual And Accessibility Review

### Findings

- The user correctly challenged the evidence standard: first-viewport screenshots do not prove long WinUI pages are visually acceptable in lower sections.
- Existing functional automation can drive offscreen controls through the accessibility tree, but visual claims still need screenshot evidence for the relevant region.
- The current evidence set is mixed: Home, Save Lab start, Media, Asset Library, Lore, Settings, and About have first-viewport screenshots; Save Editor and Patch Bench have separate scrolled workflow screenshots; Media interaction and real texture preview have focused interaction screenshots.
- The scrolled Save Editor screenshot exposed a concrete product issue: the primary patch output log repeated the full local output path after a copied-save patch.
- The earlier dotnet lock failure in this wave came from running a WinUI build and UiTest command concurrently; this matches the repo's known shared `obj/` lock rule and confirms validation should stay serial.

### Fixes Completed

- Added public-safe review note `release/readiness/winui_scrolled_visual_accessibility_review_2026-05-06.md`.
- Built a local ignored contact sheet under `subagents/winui-scrolled-visual-accessibility-review/2026-05-06/` for visual review only.
- Redacted Save Editor success output so the primary result banner/log says the copied save was patched to the selected output file without printing full input/output paths.
- Extended the copied-save editor smoke to assert the primary output log does not expose the copied input or output paths.
- Updated the copied-save editor smoke to scroll the output log into view before capturing the screenshot.

### Remaining Risks

- This pass improves and corrects evidence quality; it is not a complete screen-reader, tab-order, or keyboard-only audit.
- Lore long-document scrolled sections, tables, and code blocks still deserve a deeper scrolled visual pass.
- Broader media/save/model row coverage, full native 3D rendering, and signed installer/MSIX proof remain unproven.

### Next Inspection Target

Continue with deliberate accessibility/keyboard audits for WinUI long pages, model-renderer pipeline work, installer/MSIX planning, or deeper RE logic reconstruction.

### Remaining Risks

## Wave 157 - WinUI Asset Library Goodies Browser Filters

### Findings

- The Asset Library could show Goodies and route selected rows to model/artwork/video-aware previews, but the tab still behaved like one long catalog list.
- The user supplied screenshots of the retail Goodies wall and model viewer. Those screenshots are useful product direction, but they are runtime observation, not proof that static catalog rows reconstruct every unlock rule or in-game viewer behavior.
- The existing focused Asset Library UIA smoke already loads the ignored full-install catalog and representative Goodies samples, making it the right regression hook for a low-risk browser filter.
- The Windows/Codex lane should keep recording operating lessons as code changes happen: `rg` is unreliable here, native WinUI tests need maximized windows and scrolling/automation IDs, and catalog evidence must not be overclaimed as runtime proof.

### Fixes Completed

- Added Goodies-only filters: `All`, `Wall`, `Hidden`, `Models`, `Artwork`, and `Videos`.
- The Goodies filter strip is hidden on other Asset Library tabs and uses existing WinUI tab-button styling.
- The `Models` filter narrows to rows with model references, `Hidden` narrows to rows not mapped to the known source Goodies wall, `Artwork` narrows texture-only non-video rows, and `Videos` narrows video-linked rows.
- Extended the focused native UIA smoke so it first failed on missing `AssetGoodiesModelsFilterButton`, then passed after implementation by exercising model Goodies and hidden artwork Goodies.
- Updated public-safe release/readiness evidence, repo `AGENTS.md`, and `roadmap/agent-workflow.md` with the durable lessons from this wave.

### Remaining Risks

- This is still static catalog/save-state browsing. It does not prove the retail Goodies wall animation, every unlock criterion, or the in-game model viewer loop.
- The WinUI model viewer remains wireframe/export-based; textured, lit, animated 3D rendering remains future work.
- Broader runtime RE, including copied-profile runtime observation of Goodies behavior, remains a later lane after static RE and product surfaces are stable.

### Next Inspection Target

Continue with Goodies/static RE coverage, native Asset Library visual polish from maximized screenshots, full textured model-renderer planning, or deeper Ghidra/source mapping of Goodies unlock and viewer behavior.
