## Wave 160 - Goodies Filter Status Copy

### Findings

- The Goodies filter strip narrowed catalog rows, but it did not visibly state what the active filter proves.
- The hidden Goodies filter especially needed a product-facing caveat: hidden means shipped rows without a known wall coordinate, not runtime reachability proof.
- A first UIA assertion correctly failed on missing `AssetGoodiesFilterStatus`.

### Fixes Completed

- Added a Goodies-only filter status line under the filter strip.
- The `Models` filter status now says it is showing model Goodies from static catalog links and that textured in-game model viewing remains separate runtime work.
- The `Hidden` filter status now says it is showing shipped Goodies without a known wall coordinate and explicitly says this is static catalog evidence, not runtime reachability.
- Extended the focused maximized Asset Library UIA smoke to assert the model and hidden filter status text.

### Remaining Risks

- The status copy protects the claim boundary, but runtime Goodies reachability still requires copied-profile/windowed runtime proof.
- Textured, lit, animated in-app Goodies model viewing remains future work.

### Next Inspection Target

Continue with static Goodies RE consistency, Ghidra/source read-back, or native visual review of the Asset Library Goodies tab after the new status line.

## Wave 161 - Goodies Read-Only Static RE Probe Refresh

### Findings

- The current Goodies product/status work raised the need for a fresh static RE baseline.
- Existing repo probes can refresh source/static, installed mission-script, and existing Ghidra-export evidence without launching BEA, touching the installed executable, or mutating a Ghidra project.

### Fixes Completed

- Reran `goodies_runtime_readback_probe.py --check`: `15/15` groups passing.
- Reran `goodies_source_access_probe.py --check`: source Goodie API lines `set=3`, `get=3`, direct `71-73=0`.
- Reran `goodies_script_corpus_probe.py` against the installed Steam mission-script corpus: `733` scripts, `32` Goodie calls, indices `51,53,68,69,70,71`, target `72-74=0`.
- Reran `goodies_ghidra_readback_probe.py --check`: existing Ghidra exports show functions `6/6`, instruction contexts `8/8`, unlock read-back PASS, field map PASS.
- Recorded the public-safe probe refresh in `release/readiness/goodies_re_coverage_map_2026-05-07.md`.

### Remaining Risks

- This is read-only static/source/export evidence. It does not prove live runtime reachability for hidden/non-grid Goodies.
- Future runtime Goodies proof must use a copied profile and windowed patched copy, not the installed executable.

### Next Inspection Target

Continue with bounded runtime-proof planning for Goodies, or continue static RE where source/Ghidra names and AppCore rules still need tighter cross-checks.

## Wave 162 - Goodies Startup Filter Status Visual Fix

### Findings

- The maximized primary-screen visual smoke caught a real startup-state mismatch on the Asset Library Goodies subtab.
- The screenshot showed one visible Goodie row while `AssetGoodiesFilterStatus` still said `Showing 0 cataloged Goodies`.
- The bug only appeared on the initial Goodies route after catalog load; existing interaction smoke could switch filters and read later status text without proving the startup count was fresh.

### Fixes Completed

- Added a focused visual-smoke assertion that the Goodies screenshot route must show `1 cataloged Goodies` for the deterministic fixture catalog.
- Updated `AssetLibraryPage.UpdateAssetList()` to refresh the Goodies filter status after the item source is assigned when the selected kind is Goodies.
- Re-ran the maximized WinUI visual smoke and inspected the regenerated ignored screenshot at `subagents/winui-visual-qa/2026-05-05/07-asset-library-goodies.png`.

### Lessons Recorded

- Keep screenshot review in the loop even when UIA interaction tests pass. Visual evidence caught stale copy that was technically present and accessible but wrong for the visible list.
- Startup/deep-link screen state needs its own assertions. A test that clicks into a later state can miss an initialization bug.
- Maximized screenshots remain the default for broad WinUI surface review; laptop first-viewport captures are useful only when explicitly testing narrow-window behavior.

### Remaining Risks

- The fix proves the deterministic visual-smoke Goodies startup count. It does not prove every private full-install Goodies filter count or runtime Goodies wall replay.
- Full textured/animated model viewing and copied-profile runtime Goodies reachability remain later work.

### Next Inspection Target

Continue with native Asset Library visual polish from maximized screenshots, or shift back into static/copy-profile Goodies RE where runtime reachability remains unproven.

## Wave 163 - Real-Catalog Goodies Visual Coverage

### Findings

- The existing real-catalog visual smoke captured a representative model row, but it did not capture the Goodies browser states that answer the user's current question about actual extracted Goodies content.
- Full-install Goodies model and artwork rows are backed by generated catalog links and local exports. Video Goodies are catalog-linked but currently tell users to use Media playback instead of offering an in-place handoff.
- The first version of the new smoke failed on exact visible-text expectations and an overly narrow hidden-artwork phrase. The screenshots and assertion output clarified the actual UI copy.

### Fixes Completed

- Added `AssetLibrary_CapturesRealGoodiesBrowserWhenCatalogProvided`, gated by `ONSLAUGHT_WINUI_REAL_ASSET_CATALOG`.
- The smoke captures ignored screenshots for:
  - `asset-library-real-goodie-model.png`
  - `asset-library-real-goodie-artwork.png`
  - `asset-library-real-goodie-video.png`
- The smoke asserts a real model Goodie has an extracted model export and wireframe preview, a hidden artwork Goodie has an extracted artwork export and explicit wall non-exposure text, and a video Goodie is linked to the media catalog without enabling a fake export action.

### Lessons Recorded

- Full-catalog visual proof needs representative Goodies rows, not only generic texture/model rows.
- Exact visible-text checks should use the actual rendered heading where the helper uses exact UIA text matching.
- Video Goodies need a future WinUI product handoff into Media playback; current behavior is honest but not yet low-friction.

### Remaining Risks

- Screenshots prove representative Goodies model/artwork/video states, not every Goodie row.
- Video Goodies still require the user to switch to Media manually.
- Runtime replay of the live Goodies wall and textured/animated in-app model viewing remain unproven.

### Next Inspection Target

Either add a Media handoff for video Goodies, improve the Goodies browser toward a wall/grid presentation, or continue static/copy-profile RE for Goodies runtime reachability.

## Wave 164 - Video Goodie Media Handoff

### Findings

- The real-catalog Goodies visual coverage exposed a concrete product friction point: video Goodies were honest about their Media linkage, but the user still had to switch to Media and search manually.
- The first focused UIA expansion failed because `AssetOpenInMediaButton` did not exist, proving the source row had no direct handoff.
- After adding the handoff, the smoke still failed because Media searched `UsTheMovie` but did not settle the selected-player panel. The maximized debug screenshot showed the list row and footer selected `Credits Video` while the Now Playing card reverted to `No video selected`.
- Root cause: WinUI TreeView selection events can report transient group/null states during programmatic filtering and navigation. Those transient events were clearing the selected video after the real selection.

### Fixes Completed

- Added a small `MediaHandoffService` for in-process WinUI handoff requests.
- Added `Open in Media` for video Goodies; the button is only enabled when a linked video sequence exists.
- Media video search now matches file stems as well as human titles/sections, allowing `UsTheMovie` to find `Credits Video`.
- Media handoff now resolves a matching `.vid` from the configured read-only install if the regular tree is not ready yet, using `MediaCatalogService.GetMainVideoDisplayName()` for consistent human naming.
- Video TreeView selection no longer clears the selected player for transient null/group focus events; the selected player remains stable unless a filter mismatch or library reset actually invalidates it.
- The real-catalog Goodies smoke now clicks `Open in Media`, waits for `Credits Video`, asserts `UsTheMovie.vid` in the source summary, and captures `asset-library-real-goodie-video-media-handoff.png` under ignored `subagents/` evidence.

### Lessons Recorded

- Cross-page handoffs need destination-state assertions, not only source-button assertions. Search text alone is not proof that the target workflow is usable.
- Native TreeView group/null selection events can be transient during filter/navigation handoffs. Preserve the current selected media item unless the filter or library state truly invalidates it.
- Filtered UIA tests can rebuild the test assembly without rebuilding the WinUI app executable; run the WinUI build before UIA smoke after app code changes.

### Remaining Risks

- The handoff proves selection of one representative real video Goodie (`Goodie 077 - Development` -> `UsTheMovie` -> `Credits Video`), not every video Goodie row.
- It does not prove Bink playback or conversion from the Goodie row itself; playback remains in the Media page and broader packaged/runtime playback coverage remains separate.
- Runtime Goodies wall replay, unlock animation, and in-game Goodies viewer behavior remain unproven.

### Next Inspection Target

Continue with broader Asset Library/Media coverage, Goodies wall/grid product polish, or static/copy-profile RE for hidden/non-grid Goodies reachability. Keep installed game assets read-only.

## Wave 165 - Durable Agent Lessons Ledger

### Findings

- Lessons from the long WinUI/RE campaign were present but scattered across `AGENTS.md`, `roadmap/agent-workflow.md`, lore-book mirrors, and this progress ledger.
- Future agents need one durable place to learn from repeated mistakes: unreliable `rg`, terminal/process pressure, stale generated-artifact loops, native UIA viewport issues, user-input smoke contamination, media handoff state, Goodies proof boundaries, and release/public-safety traceability.
- Adding a public roadmap doc made release allowlist/profile artifacts legitimately stale; the release checks caught the change before commit.
- Direct `npm.cmd` spawning from the current Node REPL returned `spawn EINVAL`, while `cmd.exe /d /s /c npm run ...` worked. The command issue was tooling invocation-specific, not a repo test failure.
- A final doc-command pass caught evidence prose that looked like a fake npm script invocation. The wording was fixed to avoid creating a fake documented script reference.

### Fixes Completed

- Added `roadmap/agent-lessons-learned.md` and `lore-book/roadmap/agent-lessons-learned.md` as mirrored durable operating lessons.
- Linked the lessons note from both `roadmap/agent-workflow.md` and `lore-book/roadmap/agent-workflow.md`.
- Added the lessons note to the `AGENTS.md` key-path index.
- Regenerated release manifest/profile outputs after the new public docs were added.

### Lessons Recorded

- Lessons should be promoted out of transient chat summaries and state ledgers when they affect future operating safety or proof quality.
- Generated release artifacts should be regenerated only after checks prove they are stale, and the reason should be recorded.
- Long-lived execution environments can have their own command-spawn quirks; rerun through a stable shell wrapper before treating an invocation failure as a repo failure.
- Evidence prose can accidentally look like a command. Avoid npm-script-looking phrasing unless it is an actual root script invocation.
- Keep lessons precise and evidence-backed. Do not turn `AGENTS.md` into a long narrative when a focused linked doc can hold the detail.

### Remaining Risks

- The lessons ledger is documentation only. It does not close the remaining RE/product gaps around runtime Goodies behavior, full asset reconstruction, signed WinUI packaging, or broader packaged-runtime proof.
- Future agents still need to keep adding lessons when new failure modes appear.

### Next Inspection Target

Return to the active WinUI/RE campaign: static Goodies wall/grid mapping verification, real asset extraction breadth, or a designed Goodies browser product slice. Avoid installed-game mutation.

## Wave 166 - Goodies Wall Mapping Invariant Guard

### Findings

- `GoodieWallGridMappingService` already had sampled coordinate, hidden-slot, and slot-label tests.
- The missing guard was whole-map coverage: every visible index should round-trip from `Locate(index)` to the recovered `Resolve(x, y)` mapping, and every compiled visible coordinate should resolve to a unique index.
- This matters before any Goodies wall/grid browser work because the product surface should not drift from the recovered retail `get_goodie_number` truth.

### Fixes Completed

- Added `GoodieWallGridMappingService_LocatesEveryVisibleIndexWithCompiledRoundTrip`.
- Added `GoodieWallGridMappingService_CompiledVisibleCoordinatesAreUnique`.
- Updated `release/readiness/goodies_grid_mapping_appcore_2026-05-07.md` with the stronger invariant and validation results.

### Lessons Recorded

- Sampled RE tests are useful, but recovered lookup tables deserve whole-table invariants when the state space is small and bounded.
- Guard UI-facing RE services before building richer UI on top of them.
- The Goodies wall has 230 source-grid-visible indices in the known mapping: displayable `0..232` minus shipped-but-hidden `71..73`.

### Remaining Risks

- This is still static AppCore proof. It does not replay the live Goodies wall, unlock animation, or in-game viewer runtime behavior.
- It does not extract or render textured/animated models.

### Next Inspection Target

Continue with Goodies product planning only after design approval, or stay in static RE by checking asset catalog completeness and source/runtime access probes against the current full-install catalog.

## Wave 167 - Asset Library Backing Truth Refresh

### Findings

- The existing private full-install Goodies and asset probes directly answer whether the current Asset Library rows are real extracted/cataloged data or samples.
- Fresh public-safe probes show Goodie archives `232 / 233` displayable slots, unique Goodie refs resolved textures `206/206` and meshes `42/42`, Goodie catalog linkage textures `194/194`, models `45/45`, and videos `34`.
- Source/runtime-static probes also stay green: source Goodie API lines set `3`, get `3`, direct 71-73 source API calls `0`; runtime-static token groups `15/15`; mission-script corpus `733` scripts, `32` Goodie calls, and no target script indices `72-74`.

### Fixes Completed

- Added `release/readiness/winui_asset_library_backing_truth_2026-05-07.md`.
- The new public-safe note separates actual retail extraction, generated catalog/export data, source/reference evidence, WinUI product behavior, and still-unproven runtime behavior.

### Lessons Recorded

- Answer asset/product provenance questions with explicit buckets: retail install, generated catalog, exported private artifact, source/reference evidence, sample/mock data, and runtime proof.
- Do not let working preview paths imply complete textured/animated model viewing or runtime Goodies proof.

### Remaining Risks

- The current WinUI model surface remains wireframe/export-based, not a textured/animated viewer.
- Runtime Goodies wall replay, Goodies 71-73 reachability, and full game reconstruction remain unproven.

### Next Inspection Target

Choose between a designed Goodies browser/grid product slice, a textured model-viewer hardening slice, or copied-profile runtime Goodies wall proof. Do not mutate the installed game.

## Wave 168 - WinUI Maximized Startup

### Findings

- `MainWindow` restored a saved native window size, and UI/visual smokes maximized the app manually, but the product app itself did not start maximized by default.
- The user confirmed maximized startup is better for real use and screenshot surface area.

### Fixes Completed

- Added `MainWindow.MaximizeForUserWorkspace()` using WinUI `OverlappedPresenter.Maximize()`.
- `App.OnLaunched` now activates the main window and then maximizes it.
- Added a product-lane static guard and tightened the live launch smoke so it asserts `WindowVisualState.Maximized`.
- Added `release/readiness/winui_maximized_startup_2026-05-07.md`.

### Lessons Recorded

- If visual QA and manual operation both prefer maximized workspace, make that the app default rather than only a test harness behavior.
- Runtime UIA window-state assertions are stronger than static code-string checks for startup window behavior.

### Remaining Risks

- Long pages still require explicit scrolled-section proof.
- Narrow-window behavior still needs separate responsive/viewport testing when that is the target.

### Next Inspection Target

Continue WinUI product hardening or static RE. Good candidates: textured model-viewer proof design, Goodies wall/grid design approval, or copied-profile runtime Goodies wall proof.

## Wave 169 - Maximized Real-Catalog Asset Smoke Refresh

### Findings

- After the maximize startup change, the private full-catalog Asset Library smokes needed to be rerun to confirm real model/texture/Goodies screens still work in the new default window state.
- The first rerun attempt failed before launching because this Node REPL does not expose `process.env`; rerunning through `cmd.exe /d /s /c set ONSLAUGHT_WINUI_REAL_ASSET_CATALOG=...&& dotnet test ...` worked.

### Fixes Completed

- Reran `AssetLibrary_CyclesRepresentativeRealRowsWhenCatalogProvided` against the private full catalog: PASS `1/1`.
- Reran `AssetLibrary_CapturesRealGoodiesBrowserWhenCatalogProvided` against the private full catalog: PASS `1/1`.
- Updated the public-safe backing-truth readiness note and the durable lessons ledger.

### Lessons Recorded

- Use a shell wrapper for private environment variables when the persistent Node REPL does not expose `process.env`.
- Re-run representative real-catalog UI smokes after window-management changes, because viewport/state changes can affect selection, scrolling, and screenshots.

### Remaining Risks

- These smokes still prove representative rows, not every catalog row.
- They do not prove textured/animated in-app 3D model rendering or runtime Goodies wall replay.

### Next Inspection Target

Continue with either a designed textured model-viewer/product slice or copied-profile runtime Goodies wall proof.

## Wave 170 - Durable Lessons Refresh

### Findings

- The lessons ledger already captured major WinUI/native UIA, search/terminal, release, and RE proof boundaries, but the latest steering clarified that future agents need even stronger handoff discipline.
- The same conversation also clarified a recurring product-truth risk: real extracted/cataloged assets are not the same claim as a complete textured model viewer, runtime Goodies wall proof, or from-scratch game rebuild.

### Fixes Completed

- Expanded `roadmap/agent-lessons-learned.md` and the synchronized `lore-book/roadmap/agent-lessons-learned.md` mirror.
- Added durable notes on compaction resume, chat-summary authority, repeated user corrections, current-capability versus proof categories, representative private-catalog smokes, Goodies Browser design gating, and extracted-data versus full-rendered-product claims.

### Lessons Recorded

- After compaction, verify the active goal, branch/head, latest commits, state files, and next inspection target before continuing.
- Keep current capability, representative proof, runtime proof, and future product slices separate in state/evidence.
- Do not turn user steering about Goodies/model aspirations into a new visual feature implementation without an explicit design and test slice.

### Remaining Risks

- This documentation wave improves future-agent behavior but does not close runtime Goodies proof, textured/animated model rendering, or full static reconstruction gaps.

### Next Inspection Target

Resume hardening around `AssetModelTextureLinkService` and its tests before any richer model-viewer product slice is designed.

## Wave 171 - Asset Model Texture Link Hardening

### Findings

- `AssetModelTextureLinkService` already matched catalog textures by canonical ref, export filename, and compact normalized keys.
- It did not filter blank/whitespace texture binding strings before displaying/counting them. A malformed or partial FBX summary could therefore inflate binding counts and make model-link copy less honest.

### Fixes Completed

- Filtered blank/whitespace model texture binding names before sorting, displaying, and catalog matching.
- Added `ModelTextureLinks_IgnoresBlankBindingsAndMatchesPathVariants`.
- Normalized both slash styles in `FbxModelSummaryReader` before extracting texture filenames from FBX string properties.
- Added `release/readiness/asset_model_texture_link_hardening_2026-05-07.md`.

### Validation

- Focused model texture-link tests passed: `2/2`.
- Focused FBX model-summary reader tests passed: `3/3`.
- Full AppCore tests passed: `80/80`.
- Full private generated catalog model-preview coverage passed after the hardening: `352/352` model rows with existing exports, metadata, wireframes, texture bindings, and at least one catalog-matched texture link; unmatched rows `0`; total texture binding nodes `8448`; total catalog-matched texture links `1268`.

### Lessons Recorded

- Model-preview foundation work should harden parser/link correctness before adding richer rendering UI.
- Empty extraction metadata should be filtered before user-facing counts are computed.
- FBX string paths should be normalized independent of platform separator behavior before extracting public-safe filenames.
- Row-level model texture coverage does not mean total material completion. Placeholder/default FBX bindings need explicit handling before a textured model viewer can be honestly claimed.

### Remaining Risks

- Full-install model texture-link coverage is now refreshed at summary level, but raw row payloads remain private and this still does not prove textured/animated in-app model rendering.
- This does not add textured or animated in-app model rendering.

### Next Inspection Target

Run docs/release/state validation, then continue with either deeper model-link coverage against the private catalog or a copied-profile runtime Goodies proof plan.

## Wave 179 - Targeted Goodies Copied-Save Setup Helper

### Findings

- `BesFilePatcher` already has a broad Goodies patch path that writes displayable slots 0-232, which includes Goodies 71-73.
- The planned hidden/non-grid runtime proof benefits from a narrower setup primitive that can alter only copied Goodies 71-73 instead of relying on the broad all-Goodies product patch.

### Fixes Completed

- Added `BesFilePatcher.PatchGoodieStates(inputPath, outputPath, statesByIndex)` for copied-save proof setup.
- The helper refuses in-place writes, validates fixed save size/version, writes true-view Goodie offsets, permits displayable indices 0-232 only, permits raw states 0-3 only, and preserves neighboring/reserved slots unless explicitly targeted.
- Added focused AppCore tests proving Goodies 71-73 can be set while preserving slots 70/74/233 and proving reserved slot 233 is rejected.
- Updated the Goodies 71-73 runtime proof plan and active/lore quick references to point future runtime proof at the targeted helper while keeping it clearly labeled as setup, not runtime proof.

### Validation Commands

- `dotnet test OnslaughtCareerEditor.AppCore.Tests/OnslaughtCareerEditor.AppCore.Tests.csproj --nologo --filter PatchGoodieStates` - RED first, failed with missing `BesFilePatcher.PatchGoodieStates`.

- `dotnet test OnslaughtCareerEditor.AppCore.Tests/OnslaughtCareerEditor.AppCore.Tests.csproj --nologo` - PASS, 82/82 tests.
- `dotnet build OnslaughtCareerEditor.WinUI/OnslaughtCareerEditor.WinUI.csproj --nologo` - PASS, 0 warnings/errors.
- `cmd.exe /c npm run test:md-links` - PASS.

- `py -3 tools/docsync_check.py` - PASS.
- `py -3 tools/release_profile_snapshot.py --check` - PASS, R0=1355 R2=0 R3=2 R4=18186.

- `cmd.exe /c npm run test:public-allowlist` - PASS, 1326 rows checked.
- `cmd.exe /c npm run test:repo-hygiene` - PASS.

### Remaining Risks

## Wave 184 - CLI Goodie State Proof Setup

### Findings

- AppCore already had a focused `BesFilePatcher.PatchGoodieStates` helper for copied-save Goodie state setup, but the active C# CLI did not expose it.
- Runtime Goodies 71-73 proof should not rely on one-off byte edits when a typed helper can prepare copied saves through true-view offsets.

- Added `--set-goodie-state INDEX:STATE` to the C# CLI.

- Routed the option through `BesFilePatcher.PatchGoodieStates`.
- Added a narrow-mode guard so `--set-goodie-state` cannot be combined with broad rank, kill, settings, options, keybind, or all-Goodies patch options.
- Added `release/readiness/cli_goodie_state_override_2026-05-07.md`.
- Updated the Goodies 71-73 copied-profile runtime proof plan to use the CLI helper.
- Added a durable lesson to prefer safe targeted AppCore helpers over ad hoc copied-save byte edits.

## Wave 203 - Goodies Scalar Reference Scan

### Findings

- A full-program instruction scalar search for `0x47`, `0x48`, and `0x49` is too noisy to treat as direct reachability evidence.
- The new summarizer separates known Goodies support functions, stack/member offset noise, literal-immediate rows, and focused Goodies/frontend/script/career-adjacent candidates.
- Current broad output has 1698 scalar rows, 11 known support rows, 169 literal-immediate candidate rows, and 41 focused candidates. None is self-evident proof of a hidden Goodies selector.

- Added read-only Ghidra script `tools/ExportScalarReferences.java`.

- Updated release checklist, Goodies quick-reference mirrors, hidden proof plan, lessons mirrors, and state files.

### Remaining Risks

### Next Inspection Target

## Wave 212 - Model UV Mapping Metadata

### Findings

- `FbxModelSummaryReader` already recorded vertices, polygon indices, material nodes, texture nodes, and texture filenames, but it did not record UV coordinate/index metadata.
- Future textured-renderer work needs this static metadata before any honest claim about mapping textures onto exported model geometry.

### Fixes Completed

- Added `TextureCoordinateCount` and `TextureCoordinateIndexCount` to `AssetModelSummary`.
- Updated the binary FBX reader to count `UV` coordinate pairs and `UVIndex` entries from `LayerElementUV`-style nodes without trying to infer renderer parity.
- Added `RowsWithTextureCoordinates`, `RowsWithTextureCoordinateIndices`, `TotalTextureCoordinates`, `TotalTextureCoordinateIndices`, and per-row UV counts to `AssetModelPreviewCoverage`.
- Updated the WinUI Asset Library coverage summary and selected-model facts panel to show UV mapping metadata through `AssetModelUvCount`.

## Wave 214 - Model Connection Metadata

### Findings

- AppCore now records UV and material-layer metadata, but it did not expose FBX connection nodes that show whether texture objects are linked to material objects.
- Future textured-renderer work needs static connection evidence before any honest claim about texture-to-material mapping readiness.

### Fixes Completed

- Added `ObjectConnectionCount`, `PropertyConnectionCount`, and `TextureToMaterialConnectionCount` to `AssetModelSummary`.
- Updated the binary FBX reader to read long properties, remember Material/Texture object ids, count `OO` and `OP` connection nodes, and classify `OP` Texture-to-Material links when object ids resolve.
- Added rows/totals for object, property, and texture-to-material connections to `AssetModelPreviewCoverage`.
- Updated the WinUI Asset Library coverage summary and selected-model facts panel to show texture-material link metadata through `AssetModelConnectionCount`.
- Added `release/readiness/winui_model_connection_metadata_2026-05-08.md` and updated release/extraction docs with the static-metadata boundary.

## Wave 216 - Model Material Slot Coverage Summary

### Findings

- Wave 215 preserved readable texture-to-material slot names per model, but the coverage summary did not aggregate slot-name coverage across loaded model rows.
- The private full-install Asset Library breadth smoke could prove the real generated catalog surfaces that summary, as long as the assertion remains public-safe and avoids committing private catalog data.

### Fixes Completed

- Added `RowsWithTextureToMaterialSlotNames` and distinct `TextureToMaterialSlotNames` to `AssetModelPreviewCoverage`.
- Added per-row `TextureToMaterialSlotNames` to `AssetModelPreviewCoverageRow`.
- Updated the WinUI Asset Library coverage summary to report material-slot row coverage and a short slot-name sample.
- Tightened the private full-corpus Asset Library UIA breadth smoke to assert `352/352 model rows report material slots`.
- Added `release/readiness/winui_model_slot_coverage_summary_2026-05-08.md` and updated release/extraction docs with the static-metadata boundary.

### Validation Commands

- `dotnet test .\OnslaughtCareerEditor.AppCore.Tests\OnslaughtCareerEditor.AppCore.Tests.csproj --nologo --filter "FullyQualifiedName~AssetCatalogServiceTests.ModelPreviewCoverage_SummarizesWireframeAvailability"` before implementation - FAIL/expected red; missing aggregate slot-name coverage fields. ## Wave 217 - Model Slot Host Diagnostic

## Wave 218 - Goal Completion Audit

### Findings

- The WinUI-first repo structure and recent asset/Goodies evidence are strong, but the full campaign objective is not complete.
- Current proof is strongest for repo/lane organization, public-safe release posture, static asset extraction coverage, Goodies catalog coverage, and model metadata/texture linkage.
- Current proof remains partial for native textured model rendering, runtime Goodies model-viewer parity, packaged WinUI release proof, broader media playback proof, full gameplay logic coverage, and rebuild coverage.
- A native model-viewer implementation would be new UI/rendering behavior and should go through design approval instead of being improvised.

### Fixes Completed

- Added `.codex/state/winui-product-re-campaign-completion-audit-2026-05-08.md` mapping the active goal to concrete artifacts, status, gaps, and next options.

### Validation Commands

- State JSON parse - PASS; `developer_agent_state.json` and `documentation_agent_state.json` parsed successfully.
- `git diff --check` - PASS.

### Remaining Risks

- The audit is a planning/state artifact, not product proof. It intentionally does not claim completion.

### Next Inspection Target

Prefer static material semantics reduction before a native textured-model viewer unless the user explicitly approves a viewer design spike.

## Wave 219 - Model Texture Placeholder Filter

### Findings

- AppCore treated FBX template/default texture placeholders such as `default10.png` and `base_color_texture.png` as real missing texture bindings.
- The Python model texture linkage probe matched texture catalog `canonical_ref` values but not exported filenames or compact spacing/case variants, so older evidence overstated sidecar-only rows for the current full-install catalog.
- Current private full-install static model texture evidence now resolves all real checked model texture refs through sidecars and catalog rows.

### Fixes Completed

- Updated `AssetModelTextureLinkService` to ignore template/default texture placeholders before classifying missing catalog texture bindings.
- Added AppCore coverage for placeholder filtering and path/export-name variants.
- Updated `tools/model_texture_linkage_probe.py` to index catalog canonical refs, export filenames, and compact variants.
- Updated probe fixture coverage to prove sidecar fallback still works while export filename matching prevents false catalog-missing rows.
- Added `release/readiness/winui_model_texture_placeholder_filter_2026-05-08.md` and updated release/extraction docs with the corrected current full-install counts.

### Validation Commands

- `dotnet test .\OnslaughtCareerEditor.AppCore.Tests\OnslaughtCareerEditor.AppCore.Tests.csproj --nologo --filter "FullyQualifiedName~AssetCatalogServiceTests.ModelTextureLinks_IgnoresBlankBindingsAndMatchesPathVariants"` before implementation - FAIL/expected red; `default10.png` remained in texture bindings.

## Wave 220 - Model Material Mapping Modes

### Findings

- AppCore counted material layers and material-assignment indices, but it did not preserve FBX `LayerElementMaterial` mapping/reference modes.
- A future textured model renderer needs these modes to know whether material assignments are polygon-scoped, direct, indexed, or global before mapping textures to geometry.

### Fixes Completed

- Extended `FbxModelSummaryReader` to preserve `MappingInformationType` and `ReferenceInformationType` strings under `LayerElementMaterial` nodes.
- Extended `AssetModelSummary`, `AssetModelPreviewCoverage`, coverage rows, and AppCore.Host `inspect-asset-model-preview` output with material mapping/reference mode fields.
- Added focused tests/source guards for reader, coverage, and host diagnostic exposure.
- Added `release/readiness/winui_model_material_mapping_modes_2026-05-08.md` and updated release/extraction docs.

## Wave 223 - Model Vertex Color Metadata

### Findings

- AppCore preserved normals, UVs, material mappings, and texture links, but it did not preserve FBX `LayerElementColor` counts or mapping/reference modes.
- Future native model rendering should know whether vertex-color layers exist instead of assuming texture-only shading.

- Extended `FbxModelSummaryReader` to preserve vertex-color counts, optional color-index counts, and `MappingInformationType` / `ReferenceInformationType` strings under `LayerElementColor` nodes.

## Wave 227 - Goodies Model-Viewer Runtime Proof

### Findings

- The runtime preflight was green, so the next highest-value RE gap was the live copied-profile model-viewer proof rather than another static metadata pass.
- The first live branch was a useful negative: from `Hawk Winter`, pressing RIGHT eight times selected `Race Challenge 1`, matching the known top-row mapping to Goodie 66 and opening a level-loading path rather than a model viewer.
- The positive route used the mapped model row: DOWN then RIGHT selected `BE:A Unit-01 'Pulsar'`, a model Goodie.

### Fixes / Evidence Completed

- Prepared a fresh copied profile under ignored `subagents/goodies-model-viewer-runtime-proof-2026-05-08/`.
- Verified/applied `force_windowed` only on the copied executable; it was already patched, so no bytes were written.
- Copied a valid existing 10,004-byte save into copied-profile slot `BEA 1.bes`; copied save analysis reported version `0x4BD1`, `CareerInProgress=YES`, and `232/233` displayable Goodies unlocked.
- Launched the copied profile with `-skipfmv`, loaded the copied save through the retail UI, reached Goodies, selected `BE:A Unit-01 'Pulsar'`, opened the in-game model viewer, captured before/after bounded input, and stopped the copied process.
- Private captures show the selected `Pulsar` label, rendered in-game model-viewer screen, and a changed view/text state after bounded input.
- Added public-safe evidence at `release/readiness/goodies_model_viewer_runtime_proof_2026-05-08.md` and updated the release checklist/curated manifest.

## Wave 232 - Current WinUI MSIX Signing Probe

## Wave 236 - BattleEngine Projectile Helper Stealth Scan

### Findings

- The previous handoff left an intentional WIP around `CBattleEngine__UpdateAutoTargetSetAndFireProjectiles` at `0x00406560`.
- The focused question was whether this already named target/projectile helper contains the source-style `CBattleEngine::WeaponFired` stealth reset writes, not whether retail weapon fire can never clear stealth.
- The probe confirms the helper still carries target-entry resolution, target filtering, tracked-set removal, forward-target selection, `CBattleEngine__AddProjectile`, and the known `0.01` stealth-style target-range context.

## Wave 245 - BattleEngine Weapon Event-Handler Semantic Rename

### Findings

- Stuart source still provides `CBattleEngine::WeaponFired(CWeapon*)` and weapon-fire call sites, but the checked source tree does not contain a concrete `CWeapon` implementation to map directly.
- The recovered `0x00506930` decompile is better described as an event-driven burst handler than direct source `CWeapon::Fire`: it checks event id `0x1389`, calls `CEngine__SpawnProjectileBurstFromCurrentPreset`, increments burst/event state, and reschedules through `CEventManager__AddEvent_AtTime`.
- The adjacent `0x00505f70` decompile detaches/shuts down the weapon and conditionally calls `OID__FreeObject`, matching scalar deleting destructor shape.
- Pointer-table read-back at `0x005dfc94` confirms slot 0 now points to `CWeapon__HandleFireBurstEvent` and slot 1 now points to `CWeapon__scalar_deleting_dtor`; adjacent slot 4 remains non-code in the checked table, so broad contiguous-vtable claims are still avoided.
- `0x00506010` and `0x005069f0` remain provisional behavior labels pending separate review.
